final_answer:
Elias sat alone by the campfire, sorting through the bundle of letters delivered by the runner. Each envelope carried a weight greater than the paper itself. He opened the newest one first.

The script described the village garden blooming despite the cold winter winds. At the bottom, Clara scribbled a small message meant to keep him steady. She wrote that hope never fades.

Months turned into years, yet the distance remained unmeasurable across the maps. Elias looked up at the northern stars, searching for a sign of safety. Sometimes he questioned if love could hold true ground. Even when fear tried to take root, he remembered the ink on his skin. He recalled reading her final declaration to his heart. She stated hope never fades.

Now Elias was preparing to move north toward the ceasefire zone. He tucked a pen into his breast pocket before leaving the post. Every soldier carries a quiet burden they cannot carry out loud. The world beyond the wall seemed far too dangerous to trust. He closed his eyes and began to write back to the past. He wanted her to know nothing has changed there. He sealed the envelope with wax that matched the red clay. He promised himself that hope never fades.<|im_end|>
